cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the question and the passage. The question is asking: "A lot of figures are used in the text in order to ___." The answer choices are (A) record the valuable data, (B) provide concrete evidence, (C) introduce a research method, and (D) give a detailed description. First, I need to understand why the figures are being used in the passage. Let me go through the text again to see the context of the numbers. The passage starts by mentioning that in the past two decades, China has undergone economic reforms. Then, it says that researchers at the University of Missouri found about 30 percent of Chinese urban households now own at least one credit card and that the growth rate of credit card adoption has been an average of 40 percent per year between 2004 and 2009. Then Rui Yao, an assistant professor, is quoted saying that this growth has positive and negative implications. He mentions that more than 90 percent of non-credit card users were unaware of safety issues, and more than 60 percent had little knowledge of the consequences of credit card debt. He also found that 72 percent of Chinese credit card holders were never late on their payments, only 55 percent were able to pay the full balance. And 70 percent said credit cards contributed to overspending. Now, the question is why these figures are used. The options are to record data, provide evidence, introduce a research method, or give a detailed description. Let me break down each option. Option (A): "record the valuable data" - The passage is not just recording data for the sake of it. The data is being used to support the argument or points being made about the implications of credit card use in China. Option (B): "provide concrete evidence" - This makes sense because the figures are used to support the claims made by Rui Yao. For example, when he says "we found that more than 90 percent..." or "only 55 percent were able to pay the full balance." The numbers are evidence to back up the statements and the implications. Option (C): "introduce a research method" - The passage doesn't explain how the research was conducted. It just states the findings. So this is not the purpose of the figures.
